title: LIDAR Micro Drone — Proximity Sensing and Altitude Hold
design_question: How to implement proximity sensing and stable altitude hold on a micro drone using LIDAR?
selected_approach: 250 mm quad frame, F4 flight controller (Betaflight), downward TFmini-S LIDAR (0.1–12 m, UART) for altitude hold PID loop. Forward-facing VL53L1X TOF sensors (4 directions, I2C via TCA9548A multiplexer) for proximity/obstacle avoidance. LIDAR altitude fed to Betaflight via MSP serial. Custom Arduino co-processor reads VL53L1X sensors and triggers emergency land if obstacle < 0.3 m.
rationale: TFmini-S is lightweight (5 g), accurate (±1 cm), and interfaces directly to FC serial port. VL53L1X provides 4-direction coverage with only 4 sensors via multiplexer. Betaflight's altitude hold mode (ALTHOLD) handles LIDAR input natively. Combined LIDAR + TOF coverage enables corridor navigation.
verification_notes: FACT: 'LIDAR Time-of-Flight — Point Cloud Distance Measurement' confirms d = c·Δt/2 and proximity threshold.
supporting_fact_titles: LIDAR Time-of-Flight — Point Cloud Distance Measurement
